title: arenaria tetraquetra subsp. racemosa
intro: Arenaria tetraquetra subsp. racemosa is a sub-species of the arenaria tetraquetra plant, commonly known as mountain sandwort, which is a low-growing, mat-forming perennial plant native to mountainous regions. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its anthelmintic, antiseptic, and anti-inflammatory properties, which make it effective in treating various health issues. The health benefits of arenaria tetraquetra subsp. racemosa include its ability to reduce inflammation, relieve digestive issues, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a valuable remedy for various health conditions. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and its medicinal properties can also be extracted using other methods, such as decoction or maceration.
